page: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-8 is an ARM-based general-purpose server instance belonging to the t2a family. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an ARMv8 Ampere Altra processor using the Neoverse-N1 microarchitecture running at 3.0 GHz. The instance is configured with 32 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local NVMe storage or integrated GPUs, and has no complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks place the t2a-standard-8 in the average tier for most multi-threaded and memory-bound operations. It achieves a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 14472.4 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 6000.0. Memory bandwidth is measured at 563.91 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 87.42 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 1073.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 6205.54. Cryptographic performance is also limited, with a weak Passmark CPU encryption score of 1300.22, and LLM inference speeds are poor, generating only 32.29 tokens per second on a small 135M model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture offers a resource-dense profile for workloads that scale horizontally and do not rely on x86-specific instruction sets like AVX or AMX. Given its balanced memory-to-core ratio and average performance in database operations and static web serving, this instance is suited for general-purpose workloads. These include web serving, software compilation, Redis-based caching, and multi-threaded utility processing, whereas it is poorly suited for single-threaded tasks or machine learning inference.
description: The Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-8 is an ARM-based general-purpose server featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs on an ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz, paired with 32 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPU accelerators, this instance relies on network-attached storage. Benchmark data shows average multi-core performance, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 6000.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 14472.4, alongside a static web serving throughput of 10.09 GB/sec. However, single-core metrics and cryptographic operations are weak, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 1073.0. The t2a-standard-8 provides a cost-efficient, resource-dense option for scale-out workloads. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, including web serving, Redis caching, software compilation, and multi-threaded utility tasks that do not require x86 vector extensions.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-8 is an ARM64 general-purpose server with 8 dedicated Neoverse-N1 vCPUs and 32 GB RAM, offering balanced multi-core performance for web serving and caching workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-8 is an ARM64 server with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 32 GB RAM, optimized for balanced general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance offering dedicated physical cores and balanced memory configuration for scale-out web serving and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 3.0 GHz
32 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
Average multi-threaded performance suitable for web serving and Redis caching
Lacks local SSD storage and GPU acceleration
Weak single-core and cryptographic benchmark performance
Ideal for scale-out ARM-native applications and software compilation
